# dokploy_mariadb (Resource)

A Dokploy-managed MariaDB database service. Deployed and polled on apply.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_mariadb" "db" {
  environment_id = dokploy_project.app.production_environment_id
  name           = "app-db"
  docker_image   = "mariadb:11"
  database_name  = "app"
  database_user  = "app"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `database_name` (String) Initial database name.
- `database_user` (String) Database user.
- `docker_image` (String) MariaDB image, e.g. `mariadb:11`.
- `environment_id` (String) Environment the database belongs to. Changing this forces replacement.
- `name` (String) Display name.

### Optional

- `database_password` (String, Sensitive) Database password. If omitted, the provider generates a 32-character `[a-zA-Z0-9]` password and stores it in state.
- `database_root_password` (String, Sensitive) MariaDB root password. If omitted, the provider generates a 32-character `[a-zA-Z0-9]` password and stores it in state.
- `description` (String) Description. Once set, removing this attribute does not clear it on the server.
- `env` (Map of String) Extra environment variables.
- `external_port` (Number) Host port to expose.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `app_name` (String) Internal service name (Dokploy-generated).
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (String) Status of the most recent deploy.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_mariadb.db <mariadbId>
```
